scope: FQ-19 blueprint art-consumer and contextual information pass: final HUD art and its consumers, resource vessel effects, framed top modules with the exact clock, the contextual right-band stack, and both-resolution layout QA. No mini-map art and no new gameplay mechanics.
summary: The HUD now renders the operator's blueprint instead of placeholders. A deterministic generator authors the final iron/brass HUD art (ornate 9-slice dock backplate, gem-crowned orb frames, fill mask, slot frames, glyph buttons) and ten UI ids are consumed with code-drawn fallbacks intact. The dock follows Photo 1/2 exactly: health orb, Inventory/Character glyphs, five key-numbered slots with a raised bright selection, Skills/Town Hall glyphs, attunement orb. Both orbs are masked bottom-up liquids with the full effect vocabulary: damage flash, recovery glow, low-health pulse, regeneration shimmer, outward use-pulse, and a rotating geometric core that burns bright at full attunement. The crest is a framed settlement panel (title, chip+bar+value C-L-R rows); the goal panel gained the headline/subgoal/milestone treatment; the events header shows the exact settlement clock (Day N - Phase HH:MM, ticking every real second). A contextual right-band stack announces the selected item, save toasts from the real F5 path, and a range-aware [E] Town Hall prompt - fixed priority, auto-hide, pinned dynamically below the live events panel. canvas_items stretch makes 640x360 and 1280x720 render the identical accepted composition. Resumed cleanly after the 2026-07-14 machine restart; the independent review's one must-fix (a non-mouse-event property access in HUD edit mode, inherited from the FQ-17 tranche) was fixed and re-verified in this run.
acceptance: Fresh 1280x720 and 640x360 screenshot tours match the Photo 1 composition and Photo 2 blueprint treatments; central view unobstructed
All FQ19_ACCEPTANCE_CHECKLIST.md sections pass with explicit deferrals recorded
Isolated waited Godot smoke 316/316 including seven fq19_* checks
Strict audit clean with ten consumed UI ids; pixel verifier 186/186
Every art consumer retains its procedural/text fallback
Independent review must-fix resolved (mouse-event type guard in hud._input)
validation: screenshot tours at 1280x720 and 640x360 -> regions visually inspected against all four references
independent sonnet review of the FQ-19 diff -> 1 must-fix (fixed), 3 should-fix (1 fixed, 2 accepted with rationale)
decisions: Final HUD art is generated deterministically (gen_hud_final_art.py) so it stays reviewable and regenerable; gen_ui_placeholders.py preserves existing files unless --force-placeholder
The dock backplate 9-slice doubles as the framed style for crest/goal/events so the HUD speaks one material language
The exact clock maps the 0..1 day fraction onto a readable 06:00-20:00 day / wrapping night; phase words Dawn/Day/Dusk/Night
canvas_items stretch (not per-module reflow) satisfies both required resolutions with one composition
The contextual-stack smoke check runs at the end of the suite because its real-time waits shift the live music clip-switch timing
Immediate save_shell on HUD-edit toggles is kept: the fq17 smoke asserts immediate persistence and edit mode is rare (review should-fix accepted with rationale)
Mini-map keeps the schematic panel; final map art deferred per the execution plan
Leave signable pass uncommitted: it stacks on the also-uncommitted 2026-07-14 art run and FQ-16..18 arc, which share files and need one operator commit decision
risks: Manual operator playtest checklist remains unchecked
fq09u1_live_clip_switch is timing-sensitive on cold/slow runs (one cold-start flake observed and reproduced clean)
Three uncommitted layers share files; a partial commit attempt would be error-prone
next_action: Operator reviews rendered captures, then decides the combined commit strategy for the three uncommitted layers (recommended: one combined implementation commit + one evidence commit).
